mission.title: Install and Configure Wasmcloud on Kubernetes
mission.description: wasmCloud is an open source Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) project that enables teams to build, manage, and scale polyglot Wasm apps across any cloud, K8s, or edge. Installing wasmCloud allows you to leverage its capabilities for creating scalable and portable applications without vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the wash CLI
steps.description:
Install the wasmCloud shell (wash) CLI:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wasmcloud/wasmCloud/refs/heads/main/install.sh | bash
sudo mv wash /usr/local/bin/
```
Or via Homebrew:
```bash
brew install wasmcloud/tap/wash
```

steps.title: Start wasmCloud locally
steps.description:
Start a local wasmCloud host:
```bash
wash up
```
This starts NATS, wasmCloud host, and the wadm application manager.

steps.title: Deploy to Kubernetes with the operator
steps.description:
For Kubernetes deployment, install from the repo charts:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/wasmCloud/wasmCloud.git
cd wasmCloud
kubectl create namespace wasmcloud
helm install wasmcloud-operator charts/runtime-operator --namespace wasmcloud
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
wash --version
wash get hosts

# For Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud
```

resolution.summary: wasmCloud is installed with the wash CLI for local development and optionally the Kubernetes operator for cluster deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall from Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall wasmcloud-operator -n wasmcloud
kubectl delete namespace wasmcloud
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubernetes operator
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd wasmCloud && git pull
helm upgrade wasmcloud-operator charts/runtime-operator --namespace wasmcloud
```

troubleshooting.title: wash up fails to start NATS
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure port 4222 is not in use:
```bash
lsof -i :4222
```

troubleshooting.title: Component fails to start
troubleshooting.description:
Check host logs:
```bash
wash get hosts
wash get inventory <host-id>
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed to manage it.
